headlines.title: EU Sets Target To Double Electrification Rate, Cut Oil & Gas Use
headlines.summary: The European Union has unveiled the Electrification Action Plan, aiming for electricity to account for 46% of total energy consumption within the bloc by 2040, effectively doubling the current rate. Spearheaded by the European Commission, the aggressive policy shift is a direct e

headlines.title: India Is Becoming the World's Refining Swing Producer
headlines.summary: For years, the world's swing oil producer has been Saudi Arabia. Need more crude? Riyadh opens the taps. Too much oil? Riyadh closes them. But while everyone has been watching who controls crude production, another swing producer has quietly been taking shape—one that deals in di

headlines.title: Could Somalia See The World's Next Major Oil Discovery?
headlines.summary: As the Gulf (the world’s traditional energy centre) becomes riskier, the oil industry is searching for new hydrocarbon provinces. headlines.title: The Undisputed Global Oil Superpower
headlines.summary: The newly released 2026 Statistical Review of World Energy reinforces a point that sometimes gets lost in political discussions about energy: the United States remains the world’s largest oil producer. But the size of the U.S. lead depends on what is being counted. When most peop

headlines.title: Asian LNG Spot Prices Jump 10% as Hormuz Crisis Reignites Supply Fears
headlines.summary: Spot prices of liquefied natural gas in Asia have surged by 10% in the past week to the highest level since March, as the re-escalation in the Middle East and the fresh near-halt to shipping through the Strait of Hormuz rekindled fears of supply in the world’s biggest LNG-importi

headlines.title: Pakistan Pays Highest Spot LNG Price in Four Years as Qatar Supply Falters
headlines.summary: State-controlled Pakistan LNG Ltd has bought the most expensive cargo of liquefied natural gas on the spot market in four years as the recent re-escalation of the Hormuz crisis cut off cargoes from its term supplier, Qatar. The state-owned Pakistani LNG importer has bought a carg

headlines.title: TotalEnergies Sees Stronger Q2 Profit as Refining and Oil Trading Surge
headlines.summary: TotalEnergies expects higher cash flows from its oil production and strong refining margins and oil trading results to boost second-quarter profits compared to the prior quarter, as oil prices rose and fuel markets tightened in the wake of the Iran war. “Downstream results and ca

headlines.title: India Hikes Diesel and Jet Fuel Export Tax
headlines.summary: India has hiked the export duties it will be charging on exports of diesel and jet fuel for the two weeks starting July 16, as the return of the Hormuz crisis is set to further tighten fuel markets.
